column3: Greenmeadows Drive (South) at Ocean Drive (Suburb:Port Macquarie, Region:North Coast NSW)
column14: Check signage; Use diversions
column15: NOTIFICATION OF LANE CLOSURES - Greenmeadows Drive (south) intersection with Ocean Drive Thursday 8th May 2025 - Friday 30th May 2025 What are the works?Asphalt works and pavement repairs at the Greenmeadows Drive (south) and Ocean Drive as part of the Ocean Drive Duplication project. Location of activity?The works will be located at the intersection of Ocean Drive and Greenmeadows Drive (south). Note that Greenmeadows Drive connects with Ocean Drive in two places. The northern connection is a signalised intersection and will not be impacted by these works. When is the activity scheduled to occur?Work is scheduled to commence Thursday 8th May 2025 and is expected to be completed by - Friday 30th May 2025. Construction activities will occur between the hours of 7am - 6pm Monday to Friday however the closure will remain in place for the entirety of the dates. All work is weather dependant. How will traffic be affected?There will be no right hand turn in or out of Greenmeadows Drive (south) at the intersection of Ocean Drive. Motorists exiting Greenmeadows Drive (south) wishing to head south should detour via the signalised intersection at Greenmeadows Drive (north) or head northbound and turn around using the Koala Street roundabout. Southbound motorists wishing to turn right into Greenmeadows Drive (south) should detour via the signalised intersection at Greenmeadows Drive (north) or continue southbound and turn around at the temporary roundabout at Maranatha Place and enter Greenmeadows Drive via the functioning left hand turn.  What are the impacts for residents and businesses?Residents and businesses, including visitors will not be able to turn right into Greenmeadows Drive (south) southbound from Ocean Drive. There will also be no right hand turn (southbound) out of Greenmeadows Drive (south)Increased traffic at Greenmeadows Drive (north) signalised intersection due to traffic detouring via this location Busways will retain their routes
column18: Ocean Drive Duplication ; https://www.pmhc.nsw.gov.au/Your-Council/About-us/Our-projects/Ocean-Drive-Duplication

column3: Savoy Street between Lake Road Narani Crescent(Suburb:Port Macquarie, Region:North Coast NSW)
column14: Check signage; Reduce your speed
column15: NOTIFICATION OF ROAD WORKS - Savoy Street, Port Macquarie - From Lake Road to Narani CrescentWhat are the works?We will be resurfacing Savoy Street, Port Macquarie.We have engaged our contractor Colas to carry out the works on our behalf. When is the activity scheduled to occur?Work is currently scheduled to commence Thursday 29 May 2025, between 7.00am - 6.00pm, and will take approximately 6 days to complete.This date is subject to change due to unfavourable weather - updates will be provided if this occurs. This will also be communicated through onsite VMS boards.How will traffic be affected?Savoy Street will have one lane closed, and one lane under traffic control.Reduced speed limit of 40 km/hr will be in place during the works. Access to properties will be maintained, with minor delays if construction works are in the immediate area. Traffic controllers will be on site to prioritise passengers getting on and off buses. What are the impacts for residents and businesses?Colas are asking residents to assist by not parking vehicles in the immediate construction area. There will be some construction noise with use of machinery. Every effort will be used to minimise this.Allow extra travel time, as there may be delays due to changed traffic conditions Access into and out of medical facilities will be maintained at all times

All work is weather dependent.How will traffic be affected?There will be no access to Dahlsford Drive at the intersection of Ocean Drive.Dahlsford Drive will be accessible via Pacific Drive and Livingstone Road.What are the impacts for residents and businesses?Residents and businesses, including visitors will not be able to access Dahlsford Drive from Ocean Drive.Increased traffic at Livingstone Road intersection with Pacific Drive due to traffic detouring via this locationBusways will retain their routes
column16: Detour from Ocean Drive to Dahlsford Drive via Pacific Drive and Livingstone Road

column3: Eastern Boundary Road at Eastern Boundary Bridge (Suburb:Bellangry, Region:North Coast NSW)
column14: Check signage; Use diversions
column15: How will traffic be affected?Eastern Boundary Road will be closed in both directions at the bridge only.Vehicles will be diverted to Bottle Brush Road, which reconnects to Bellangry Road - Council staff will drive this route before closing the road to ensure it is clear for vehicles.No traffic control on site, however Council staff will be on site to turn vehicles around where required. There will be some construction noise with use of machinery. Allow extra travel time, as there will be delays due to changed traffic conditions Residents will be able to get in and out via Bottle Brush Road.A walkway is to be installed for pedestrians

column15: Resealing Hindman Street from Ocean Drive To Central RoadWhen is the activity scheduled to occur?Work is scheduled for Tuesday 10, Wednesday 11 and Thursday 12 June 2025, between 7.00am - 4.00pm How will traffic be affected?The roads will be worked on successively - the road being worked on will have one lane closed and one lane open under traffic controlReduced speed limit of 40 km/hr will be in place during the works. Access to properties and businesses will be maintained, with minor delays when construction works are in the immediate area. What are the impacts for residents and businesses?Colas are asking residents to assist by not parking vehicles in the immediate construction area. There will be some noise with use of machinery. Every effort will be used to minimise this.Allow extra travel time, as there may be delays due to changed traffic conditions
